title: helictotrichon asperum var. roylei
intro: Helictotrichon asperum var. roylei, also known as Royle's oat grass or Royle's rough grass, is a type of ornamental grass native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Royle's oat grass is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
